scenarios.title: The Assignment
scenarios.subtitle: A week out. The clock is ticking.
scenarios.prompt: You've got a big assignment due in a week. How do you handle it?

choices.text: Break it into pieces and start early. I like being ahead
I'll start a few days before. Enough time without overdoing it
I know I'll procrastinate, but I always pull it off last minute
I honestly forget about it until it's almost too late

scenarios.title: The Difficult Concept
scenarios.subtitle: When the material just isn't clicking.
scenarios.prompt: You're studying something and you just don't get it. What's your move?

choices.text: Reread the material and look up other sources until it clicks
Watch a YouTube video. Someone out there explains it better
Ask a classmate or go to office hours
Skip it and hope it's not on the exam

scenarios.title: The Group Project
scenarios.subtitle: Everyone's favorite assignment.
scenarios.prompt: You're assigned a group project. What role do you usually end up playing?

choices.text: I take charge and organize everything. Someone has to
I do my part well but don't want to run the whole thing
I'm the ideas person. Great in the brainstorm, less great on the follow-through
I'm not proud of it, but I'm mostly hoping the group carries me

scenarios.title: The Exam
scenarios.subtitle: The big one is coming up.
scenarios.prompt: You've got a big exam coming up. How do you usually prepare?

choices.text: I make study guides, flashcards, and review notes well in advance
I review my notes a couple times and hope the main ideas stuck
I cram the night before. It's stressful but it works (mostly)
I kind of wing it and see what happens

scenarios.title: The Blank Page
scenarios.subtitle: Cursor blinking. Nothing written.
scenarios.prompt: You need to write a paper or essay. You're staring at a blank document. What happens?

choices.text: I outline first, then write section by section. Structure keeps me on track
I just start writing and figure out the structure as I go
I write the intro last because I never know how to start
I stare at it for way too long, write a sentence, delete it, repeat

scenarios.title: The Career Question
scenarios.subtitle: The question everyone keeps asking.
scenarios.prompt: Someone asks you 'what do you want to do after graduation?' What's going through your head?

choices.text: I have a clear plan. I know what field and what steps to take
I have a general direction but the specifics are fuzzy
I have too many interests and can't narrow it down
I try not to think about it honestly

scenarios.title: The Overwhelm
scenarios.subtitle: Everything is due at once.
scenarios.prompt: It's midterms week. Exams, papers, and a project all due within days. How are you handling it?

choices.text: I made a schedule weeks ago. Stressed but on track
I'm triaging. Figuring out what's worth the most and prioritizing
I'm doing everything at once and nothing well
I'm spiraling and considering dropping something

scenarios.title: The Skill They Want
scenarios.subtitle: Something you've been meaning to get better at.
scenarios.prompt: There's a skill you wish you were better at, for school, career, or personal interest. How do you usually approach learning new things?

choices.text: I find courses, tutorials, and books. I like learning systematically
I learn by doing. Just throw me in and I'll figure it out
I watch other people do it and pick up what I can
I keep meaning to start but I never get around to it
